title: Oil Steadies After First Drop This Week on Peace Talk Optimism
summary: Oil was steady after its first decline this week on optimism that ceasefire talks between the US and Iran will see energy flows resume through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: S&P Dow Jones Keeps MegaCap IPO Rules As Is After Consultation
summary: S&P Dow Jones Indices will keep its existing eligibility requirements for the S&P 500, rejecting proposals that would have made it faster for mega-cap companies such as SpaceX to gain rapid entry into the benchmark after going public.

title: Mythos AI Model Finds Hundreds of Vulnerabilities in Firefox
summary: Raffi Krikorian, chief technology officer at Mozilla, said that it's important identify and start to close a bunch of critical vulnerabilities to mitigate risks as AI dependence builds up. Krikorian said that a major consideration is making sure models are on your side and consumers are not living in 'someone else's intelligence.' (Source: Bloomberg)

US-Iran Talks Progress Stalls After Hezbollah Rejects Truce
summary: There was no sign of progress in ceasefire talks between the US and Iran after the worst burst of violence in weeks and as the Tehran-backed Hezbollah militia rejected on Thursday a US-brokered truce in Lebanon.

title: AI, Xerox Debt and Canada’s ‘Lost Status’ at Sohn Montreal
summary: Hedge fund managers and other investors are gathered at a conference in Montreal to pitch the next big thing, and everything from artificial intelligence, biotech and gold mining was on the table Thursday.
